days.title: В and на: being inside and on

vocab.exampleEn: I live in a big city.
The shop is on the street.
I am waiting for you on the square.
There is bread in the shop.
They are in the house.
I am here.
The shop is there.

grammar.title: В and на: being inside and on
grammar.explanation: Bulgarian shows where something is with prepositions, not with case endings. The two most common are в (in/inside) and на (on/at). Use в for being inside something enclosed: в града (in the city), в къщата (in the house), в магазина (in the shop). Use на for being on a surface or at an open place: на улицата (on the street), на площада (on/at the square), на масата (on the table). Note that the noun after the preposition often takes the definite suffix (-ът/-а, -та, -то, -те) when it is something specific. The verb 'to be' (съм, си, е, сме, сте, са) carries the meaning 'is located': Магазинът е на площада = The shop is on the square. Important spelling note: в becomes във before words starting with в- or ф- (във Варна), but for now plain в is fine.

examples.english: I am in the city.
The house is on the street.
The bread is in the shop.
We are on the square.

reading.title: Къде сме?
reading.text: — Къде си? — Аз съм в магазина на улицата. — А Иван? — Иван е там, на площада.
reading.translation: — Where are you? — I am in the shop on the street. — And Ivan? — Ivan is there, on the square.

days.speakingPrompt: Say where you and a friend are right now, using both в and на. For example: 'I am in the shop' and 'My friend is on the square.' Use the verb 'to be' (съм, е).

days.title: До and от: next to and from

vocab.exampleEn: The bank is next to the hotel.
I am coming from the shop.
The hotel is next to the square.
The bank is here.
The restaurant is next to the bank.
The pharmacy is on this side.
The hotel is near.

grammar.title: До and от: next to and from
grammar.explanation: До means 'next to / beside / by' and answers 'where?': Банката е до хотела (The bank is next to the hotel). It can also mean 'up to / as far as': до площада (as far as the square). От means 'from' and marks a starting point: Идвам от магазина (I am coming from the shop). With these prepositions there is still NO case change on the noun — only the definite suffix may appear (хотел → хотела, магазин → магазина). Compare with в/на from yesterday: в/на say where you are; от says where you started; до says what you are beside. The adverb близо (near) often pairs with до: близо до банката (close to the bank).

examples.english: The restaurant is next to the bank.
I am coming from the hotel.
The pharmacy is close to the square.
He is from the city.

reading.title: До аптеката
reading.text: — Къде е аптеката? — Аптеката е до банката, близо до площада. — А ти откъде идваш? — Идвам от хотела.
reading.translation: — Where is the pharmacy? — The pharmacy is next to the bank, close to the square. — And where are you coming from? — I am coming from the hotel.

days.speakingPrompt: Describe a small map in words: place a bank, a hotel and a pharmacy near each other using до and близо до. Then say which place you are coming from using от.

days.title: От ... до ...: from one place to another

vocab.exampleEn: The station is far.
The stop is next to the station.
The park is big.
The station is far from the hotel.
I walk from the station to the park.
The park is five minutes away.

grammar.title: От ... до ...: from one place to another
grammar.explanation: Combine от (from) and до (to/up to) to express a route or distance between two places: от гарата до парка (from the station to the park). This pair is very common for directions and distances: Вървя от хотела до площада (I walk from the hotel to the square). Note that here до means 'to / as far as', a direction goal, while yesterday it also meant 'next to'. Bulgarian relies on context: до банката can be 'next to the bank' or 'as far as the bank' depending on the verb (standing vs. going). With distances of time use на: на пет минути (five minutes away), на десет минути от гарата (ten minutes from the station). Still no case endings — the prepositions do all the work.

examples.english: I walk from the stop to the park.
The station is far from the hotel.

examples.russian: От къщата до магазина е близо.
examples.english: From the house to the shop is close.

examples.russian: Паркът е на десет минути от площада.
examples.english: The park is ten minutes from the square.

reading.title: От гарата до парка
reading.text: Вървя от гарата до парка. Паркът не е далеч, той е на пет минути. Спирката е до гарата.
reading.translation: I walk from the station to the park. The park is not far, it is five minutes away. The stop is next to the station.

days.speakingPrompt: Describe how you get from the train station to a park, using от ... до .... Say whether it is near or far (близо / далеч) and how many minutes it takes (на ... минути).

days.title: С: with, and by (transport)

vocab.exampleEn: I travel by bus.
The bus is at the stop.
The train is at the station.
I am going by car.
I come with a friend.
I travel by train to the city.
I go on foot.

grammar.title: С: with, and by (transport)
grammar.explanation: The preposition с has two main uses. First, 'with' (together with a person or thing): Идвам с приятел (I come with a friend); кафе с мляко (coffee with milk). Second, 'by means of' for transport: Пътувам с автобус (I travel by bus), отивам с кола (I go by car), с влак (by train). Note that for transport Bulgarian uses с, not на. The opposite of travelling 'by' something is пеша (on foot): Отивам пеша (I go on foot) — no preposition there. Spelling: с becomes със before words starting with с- or з- (със захар = with sugar), but plain с is correct elsewhere. As always, no case endings appear.

examples.english: I travel by train to the station.
I am going to the park with a friend.
They are coming by car.

examples.russian: Аз отивам пеша, ти с автобус.
examples.english: I am going on foot, you by bus.

reading.title: С какво пътуваш?
reading.text: — С какво отиваш до града? — Пътувам с влак. А ти? — Аз отивам с кола, с приятел.
reading.translation: — How (by what) are you going to the city? — I travel by train. And you? — I am going by car, with a friend.

days.speakingPrompt: Say how you travel to three different places (city, station, park) using с + a means of transport, and mention one place you go to пеша (on foot). Say if you go with a friend (с приятел).

days.title: Direction with в/на: going to a place

vocab.exampleEn: I am going to the shop.
I am coming to the park.
The child goes to school.
I am going to work.
I am going to the market.
They go to the hospital.
We are going to the cinema.

grammar.title: Direction with в/на: going to a place
grammar.explanation: Good news: the same prepositions в and на that mean 'in/on' also mean 'to' for direction. There is no separate 'into' form and no case change — Bulgarian uses the same words for location and destination. Use в for going into a building or enclosed place: отивам в магазина (I go to the shop), в болницата (to the hospital), в парка (to the park). Use на for activities and open/institutional places, often fixed expressions: на работа (to work), на пазар (to the market), на кино (to the cinema), на училище (to school). Many of these на-phrases drop the article (на работа, not на работата) when they mean the activity in general. Verbs of motion: отивам (go), идвам (come). Whether you are standing in a place or going to it, the preposition stays the same — only the verb changes.

examples.english: I am going to work by bus.
The children go to school.
We are coming to the hospital.
This evening we are going to the cinema.

reading.title: Сутрин в града
reading.text: Сутрин отивам на работа с автобус. Жена ми отива на пазар, а детето е на училище. Вечер отиваме на кино.
reading.translation: In the morning I go to work by bus. My wife goes to the market, and the child is at school. In the evening we go to the cinema.

days.speakingPrompt: Describe a typical day: where you go in the morning, afternoon and evening. Use отивам with в and на correctly (на работа, на пазар, в магазина, на кино), and add how you get there with с.

days.title: Asking and giving directions

vocab.exampleEn: Turn left.
The bank is to the right.
Go straight ahead.
The shop is on the corner.
I turn left.
Excuse me, where is the station?
The hotel is in the centre.

grammar.title: Asking and giving directions
grammar.explanation: Now we put the week's prepositions to work in real directions. Ask politely with Извинете, къде е...? (Excuse me, where is...?). Answer with direction adverbs: наляво (left), надясно (right), направо (straight ahead). Combine with на for landmarks: на ъгъла (on the corner), в центъра (in the centre), до банката (next to the bank). To tell someone to turn, use the verb завивам (I turn) or its polite command form завийте (turn): Завийте наляво (Turn left), Вървете направо (Go straight ahead). A typical answer chains prepositions: Вървете направо до площада, после завийте надясно (Go straight to the square, then turn right). Notice again: no case endings — на ъгъла, в центъра, до банката all use plain prepositions plus the definite suffix.

examples.english: Excuse me, where is the pharmacy?
Go straight ahead to the square.
Then turn left.

examples.russian: Банката е на ъгъла, до хотела.
examples.english: The bank is on the corner, next to the hotel.

reading.title: Извинете, къде е гарата?
reading.text: — Извинете, къде е гарата? — Вървете направо до площада, после завийте надясно. Гарата е на ъгъла. — Близо ли е? — Да, на пет минути.
reading.translation: — Excuse me, where is the station? — Go straight ahead to the square, then turn right. The station is on the corner. — Is it near? — Yes, five minutes away.

days.speakingPrompt: Role-play: a tourist asks you the way to the hotel in the centre. Give directions using направо, наляво, надясно, до and на ъгъла, and say how far it is.

days.title: Review: Places & prepositions

grammar.title: Recap: в, на, до, от, с
grammar.explanation: This week you learned to describe places and directions using prepositions only — Bulgarian has NO noun cases, so the preposition (plus the definite suffix) does all the work. Recap: в = in / into a place (в града, отивам в магазина). на = on / at, and 'to' for activities (на улицата, на работа, на кино). до = next to, or 'as far as / to' (до хотела; от гарата до парка). от = from a starting point (идвам от хотела; далеч от града). с = with a person/thing, or 'by' a means of transport (с приятел, с автобус); the opposite is пеша (on foot). Remember that location and direction use the SAME preposition — only the verb changes (съм в парка = I am in the park; отивам в парка = I go to the park). For directions use наляво, надясно, направо and завийте. Spelling helpers: в→във and с→със before similar-sounding consonants.

examples.russian: Отивам от хотела до гарата с автобус.
examples.english: I go from the hotel to the station by bus.

examples.russian: Аптеката е на ъгъла, до банката.
examples.english: The pharmacy is on the corner, next to the bank.
Go straight ahead, then turn left.

examples.russian: Паркът е близо, на пет минути от площада.
examples.english: The park is near, five minutes from the square.

reading.title: В центъра на града
reading.text: — Извинете, далеч ли е хотелът? — Не, той е в центъра, до площада. Вървете направо от гарата, после завийте надясно. — С автобус ли да отида? — Не, близо е. Отидете пеша, на десет минути.
reading.translation: — Excuse me, is the hotel far? — No, it is in the centre, next to the square. Go straight ahead from the station, then turn right. — Should I go by bus? — No, it is near. Go on foot, ten minutes away.

glossary.english: should I go / (for) me to go

days.speakingPrompt: Without notes, give a full set of directions from the train station to a hotel in the centre: use от, до, в, на, с (or пеша), and turning words (направо, наляво, надясно). Then say where three city places are relative to each other using до and близо до.
